title: Podman commands info, pull and others do not work with Podman 5.0.1 on windows using podman machine
body:
### Issue Description

On windows using rootful podman machine, if I try to pull an image:
```
podman pull alpine
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v5.0.1/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
```
or `podman info`
```
podman info
OS: windows/amd64
provider: wsl
version: 5.0.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v5.0.1/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Installs Podman 5.0.1
2. podman machine init --rootful --now 
3. podman pull alpine
4. one can also run podman info to get the same result

### Describe the results you received

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v5.0.1/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)

### Describe the results you expected

Pulled image. 

### podman info output

```yaml
podman info
OS: windows/amd64
provider: wsl
version: 5.0.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v5.0.1/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)

podman -v
podman.exe version 5.0.1

podman version
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v5.0.1/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows 10, using wsl as hypervisor.



### Additional information

```
podman pull alpine
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v5.0.1/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
PS C:\Users\podmanqe> podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
[root@DESKTOP-U7FRUQA ~]# podman pull alpine
WARN[0000] Using cgroups-v1 which is deprecated in favor of cgroups-v2 with Podman v5 and will be removed in a future version. Set environment variable `PODMAN_IGNORE_CGROUPSV1_WARNING` to hide this warning.
Error:
[root@DESKTOP-U7FRUQA ~]# podman -v
podman version 5.1.0-dev-04bd1b1a2
```

comments.body: Is this a dup of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22423? Can you check your system connections?

@Luap99 Likely not, this is windows. This one is more close to what I am seeing: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22373. 

Although I decide to open new issue to provide different steps to reproduce.
Problem is the podman-machine-wsl-os image. Latest image used as of today that being used in 5.0.2, 5.0.1 and 5.0.0 podman to create a machine prevent usage of rootful connection. In general podman > 5.0.0 always uses latest image, so no specific micro to be blamed.

I tried image from 2021-04-09 and it works.
This one image works nice: https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-wsl-os/releases/tag/v20240409161947

Can you check `systemctl status podman.service` and `systemctl status podman.socket` in WSL to see if the service reports any errors? Also check `journalctl -u podman.service` if you don't see anything wrong there.

@n1hility @baude PTAL! Looks like windows images are broken.

So this one v20240418122826 is broken.

This one works: v20240417162145.

> Can you check `systemctl status podman.service` and `systemctl status podman.socket` in WSL to see if the service reports any errors? Also check `journalctl -u podman.service` if you don't see anything wrong there.
> 
> @n1hility @baude PTAL! Looks like windows images are broken.

the latest image had the service down. Let me find image that broke it, I am close.

Some of these changes broke it:
```
aardvark-dns                   1.10.0-1.20240417165604887829.main.37.g01d8574.fc39
containers-common              0.58.0-10.fc39
containers-common-extra        0.58.0-10.fc39
podman                         5.1.0~dev-1.20240417173120067584.main.267.60e0ef3e0.fc39
podman-docker                  5.1.0~dev-1.20240417173120067584.main.267.60e0ef3e0.fc39
```
Please show the logs of the service, what is the error?

> containers-common              0.58.0-10.fc39

This is the broken update, it enabled composefs which never should have happen, https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22417


There a series of bigger problems here, first podman-next brings in broken rawhide changes. I think @lsm5 works on improving the flow for contianers-common to follow upstream changes with packit like done for our other packages (https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1960/) which should hopefully prevent us shipping bad changes like that.

Second, why are shipping daily updates from podman-next in our podman-machine WSL images? Shouldn't this be stable and not use development snapshots?

And lastly why the hell is no error message being reported, that is some real bug within podman. I still need to debug this.

> And lastly why the hell is no error message being reported, that is some real bug within podman. I still need to debug this.

c/storage returns empty errors, filled a issue for this: https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/143
I have rolled the stream back to v20240417162145. As soon as there is a fix will pull in the new one and restart updates
